concept.shortdesc: Approach loads squarely and position the forks evenly. Insert the forks fully under the pallet

conbody.p: Approach loads squarely and position the forks evenly. Insert the forks fully under the pallet before lifting.
Tilt the mast back slightly before lifting to stabilise the load. Check that the load is stable before travelling.
When stacking, approach the stack with the load at the correct height. Tilt the mast forward gently to place the load.
When removing a load from a stack, approach squarely, insert the forks under the load, and lift slightly to clear the stack before withdrawing.
On ramps and inclines, always travel with the load uphill. Never turn on a ramp or slope.
In narrow aisles, use slow speeds and heightened awareness. Sound the horn before entering the aisle.
Page 112 of 500 | CD100S-7 / CD120S-7 / CD140S-7 / CD160S-7 Operation & Maintenance Manual | DL06P Tier-4
Always follow all safety precautions when operating or maintaining this equipment.
Ensure all operators are properly trained and certified before operating the lift truck.
Regular maintenance is essential to safe and efficient operation of the lift truck.
Contact your local CROWN dealer for parts, service, and technical support.
Keep this manual with the lift truck at all times for reference by operators and technicians.
Record all service and maintenance activities in the maintenance logbook.
Only use genuine CROWN replacement parts to maintain warranty and performance.
